seo.title: AI eDiscovery Software — eDiscovery AI for Semantic Document Search
seo.description: AI eDiscovery software with semantic search across thousands of documents. eDiscovery AI that finds relevant evidence by meaning, not keywords. Starting at $30/mo.

hero.headline: AI eDiscovery: Powerful Document Search
hero.subheadline: Discover the future of legal document analysis with Dodonai's AI-Powered eDiscovery Software. Dodonai transforms your eDiscovery process, offering unparalleled efficiency and accuracy. Dodonai's AI-driven platform enables semantic searches across vast document pools, delivering precise results at unprecedented speed. With Dodonai, every document is at your fingertips, and no critical piece of evidence is ever out of reach.

educationalContent.title: What is eDiscovery AI Software?
educationalContent.description: eDiscovery AI software combines artificial intelligence with electronic discovery — the process lawyers use to collect and analyze digital data in legal cases. It quickly sorts and organizes emails, documents, and social media records, uses semantic search that understands meaning rather than just keywords, and improves through continuous learning. In short, eDiscovery AI makes sifting through digital evidence faster, more accurate, and far easier than legacy keyword-based platforms.

educationalContent.title: Semantic Search vs. Keyword Search
educationalContent.description: Traditional eDiscovery tools rely on keyword matching — you type a term and the software returns documents containing that exact word. This approach misses relevant documents that use synonyms or describe the same concept in different language. Dodonai's semantic search understands the meaning behind your query: ask for documents about 'breach of contract disputes' and the system finds relevant evidence even when that exact phrase never appears. This dramatically reduces both false positives and missed documents.

educationalContent.title: How Lawyers Use AI eDiscovery in Litigation
educationalContent.description: Litigation teams use AI eDiscovery to search and review thousands of documents in a fraction of the time manual review requires. Common workflows include identifying hot documents during early case assessment, running privilege reviews, and building chronologies from email chains and correspondence. Dodonai integrates with {{link:/deposition-summary-software/|deposition summary}} and {{link:/ai-medical-record-summaries-chronologies/|medical record summary}} workflows, giving teams a single platform for the major document-intensive tasks in litigation.

educationalContent.title: AI eDiscovery vs. Legacy Platform Costs
educationalContent.description: Legacy eDiscovery platforms like Relativity and Everlaw charge per-gigabyte hosting fees, per-user licensing, and processing surcharges that can total $50,000 or more per year for mid-size firms. Dodonai provides AI-powered semantic search, document review, and {{link:/ai-pdf-ocr/|OCR processing}} starting at $30 per month — making enterprise-grade eDiscovery accessible to solo practitioners and small firms without enterprise budgets. See our {{link:/compare/ediscovery-software-review/|eDiscovery software comparison}} for a detailed breakdown.

aiOverview.heading: Concept-Based Discovery: Search by Meaning, Not Just Keywords
paragraphs: Dodonai's eDiscovery AI goes beyond keyword matching to deliver concept-based document discovery. Describe what you're looking for in plain language — the system finds relevant documents even when they use completely different terminology. This approach eliminates the need to run dozens of keyword variations and ensures your review is comprehensive.

Every search result includes a defensibility verification loop: Dodonai shows you exactly which passages matched and why, so you can confirm relevance and document your review methodology. Combined with automated privilege detection and structured data extraction, Dodonai gives litigation teams the confidence that their document review is both thorough and defensible.

howItWorks.title: Upload your Documentary Evidence
howItWorks.description: Upload your PDF, TXT, or Word documents, or a ZIP file of these, to Dodonai. For larger file collections, our team can help by quickly uploading these documents for you.

howItWorks.title: Search Semantically
howItWorks.description: Don't worry about keyword matching. Simply approximate the type of information you want to find. Dodonai's semantic search will search your documents for similar concepts.

howItWorks.title: See Results
howItWorks.description: Dodonai will return snippets of your documentary evidence for review, identifying your hottest docs with ease.

faq.question: What is AI eDiscovery?
faq.answer: AI eDiscovery, also called eDiscovery AI, is a technology that uses artificial intelligence to enhance the process of electronic discovery in litigation. It automates and improves the searching, sorting, and analysis of digital data, making it faster and more accurate for legal teams to find relevant information across large document collections.

faq.question: How does AI improve the eDiscovery process?
faq.answer: AI significantly speeds up the eDiscovery process by quickly analyzing large volumes of data. It uses advanced algorithms to identify relevant documents more accurately, reduces manual review time, and enhances the overall quality of the discovery process.

faq.question: Can AI eDiscovery software replace lawyers or paralegals?
faq.answer: No, AI eDiscovery software is designed to assist and augment the capabilities of lawyers and paralegals, not replace them. It handles time-consuming tasks, allowing legal professionals to focus on more complex and strategic aspects of their work.

faq.question: Is AI eDiscovery secure and confidential?
faq.answer: Yes. Reputable AI eDiscovery solutions, like Dodonai, prioritize data security and confidentiality. They use encryption and other security measures to protect sensitive legal data.

faq.question: How does Dodonai's AI eDiscovery differ from other eDiscovery tools?
faq.answer: Dodonai's AI eDiscovery integrates advanced AI technology to provide smarter and more efficient data analysis. It offers semantic search capabilities and personalized user experiences, setting it apart from traditional, keyword-based eDiscovery tools. For a detailed comparison, see our {{link:/compare/ediscovery-software-review/|eDiscovery software comparison}}.

faq.question: Is Dodonai's AI eDiscovery software suitable for all sizes of law firms?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Dodonai's AI eDiscovery software is scalable and versatile, making it suitable for law firms of any size. Whether you're a solo practitioner or part of a large firm, Dodonai can be tailored to meet your specific eDiscovery needs.

faq.question: What types of documents can I search with eDiscovery AI?
faq.answer: Dodonai's eDiscovery AI supports a wide range of document types including PDFs, Word documents, emails, text files, and scanned documents. For scanned or image-based documents, Dodonai's {{link:/ai-pdf-ocr/|AI OCR}} automatically converts them to searchable text before indexing, so you can search the full text of even paper-based document productions.

faq.question: How much does AI eDiscovery cost compared to Relativity or Everlaw?
faq.answer: Legacy eDiscovery platforms like Relativity and Everlaw typically charge per-gigabyte hosting fees plus per-user licensing, easily totaling $50,000 or more per year for a mid-size firm. Dodonai starts at $30 per month with no per-gigabyte surcharges, making it accessible to firms of any size. See our {{link:/pricing/|pricing page}} for full details or read our {{link:/compare/ediscovery-software-review/|eDiscovery software comparison}}.

faq.question: Can I use eDiscovery AI with scanned or image-based PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. Dodonai's {{link:/ai-pdf-ocr/|AI-powered OCR}} processes scanned documents, faxes, and image-based PDFs automatically. Once OCR is complete, the extracted text is indexed and fully searchable using semantic search — no manual preprocessing required.

faq.question: What is eDiscovery Software?
faq.answer: eDiscovery, short for electronic discovery, refers to the process in which legal professionals identify, collect, and produce electronically stored information (ESI) as evidence in legal cases. This includes emails, documents, presentations, databases, voicemail, audio and video files, social media, and websites. The eDiscovery process is typically used in litigation, investigations, and Freedom of Information Act requests. It involves several stages, including identification, preservation, collection, processing, review, and production of digital data. eDiscovery has become increasingly important as the volume of electronic data grows and plays a crucial role in legal proceedings today.

faq.question: I have more questions. Where can I find answers?
faq.answer: Visit our {{link:https://app.dodon.ai/help|Help Center}} for detailed guides on uploading and searching documents. You can also contact us at hello@dodon.ai with any questions.
